covered_earnings_window.plain_language: Weekend run as of Saturday, June 13, 2026 at 12:03 p.m. EDT: confirmed U.S.-listed companies reporting before the next U.S. market open on Monday, June 15, 2026. No still-actionable after-close session remains before that open; Friday, June 12 after-close results were already public.

market_context_summary: U.S. indexes finished higher on Friday, June 12, with small caps leading weekly gains as oil fell on U.S.-Iran peace optimism and risk appetite improved. The thin Monday before-open earnings window is small-cap-heavy, so liquidity, bid-ask spreads, and macro risk can dominate company-specific earnings reads.

research_allocation_view.plain_statement: This is a research prioritization model, not financial advice.

consensus_method_note: Three independent passes were averaged. Disparity is a 0-100 disagreement metric. Low disparity supported AIOT and CODA; high reversal risk excluded CGC from allocation despite a stronger bearish score.
disclaimer_text: This is research, not financial advice. Earnings reactions are highly uncertain and can be driven by market positioning, guidance, macro conditions, and management commentary rather than reported results alone.
